evidence_check.claim: CR is inherent consequence of gradient-based optimization (Theorem 3.2)
evidence_check.assessment: Proof is for single gradient step with simplified transformer. COIN uses multiple steps + KL alignment — different mechanism from what theorem describes.
evidence_check.issue: Theory-to-method gap: representation-level mechanism vs prediction-level mitigation

evidence_check.assessment: ROME/MEMIT only in MQuAKE, not in AKEW/UnKEBench main benchmarks. Cannot test whether CR is NTP-specific or general to gradient-based editing.
evidence_check.issue: Comparison asymmetry weakens the diagnostic claim

severe_issues: ROME/MEMIT excluded from main benchmarks — central claim that CR is NTP-specific is untestable as designed
Theory proves representation-level binding; COIN mitigates at prediction level — mechanism gap unaddressed

strengths: CR is real and quantifiable failure mode — well-motivated
COIN practically effective (45.2% CR reduction, 23.6% editing success improvement)
Empirical validation that context prepending recovers recall is clean
Theoretical analysis provides useful conceptual frame even if assumptions are strong
weaknesses: Structured baselines not tested on main benchmarks
Theorem limited to single gradient step
